texto: Hester, meanwhile, says we should live all of life back to front. We should be born old and age younger. Our baptism should be a ritual of our funeral. We should die as infants, content in our mothers' arms, having lost all our learning and all sense of disappointment. If only we could die, she says, not knowing we'd ever grieved.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Timothy Schaffert sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. A afirmação “Hester, meanwhile, says we should live all of life back to front. We should be born old and age y…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, aging, death, útil em momentos de luto e death.

texto: Even when not in the act of writing Muscatine a letter, I was often composing one in my mind, situating the words just so, plunking one here, then one there, gauging how to sound worthy of his regard.
explicacao: Citação de Timothy Schaffert sobre temas ligados a autoestima. “Even when not in the act of writing Muscatine a letter, I was often composing one in my mind, sit…” articula autoestima, impress, impressions em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como impressions e letter.

texto: We could endlessly reminisce, live in the past to an unhealthy degree, then politely kill each other some winter night before bedtime, stirring poison into our cups of whiskey-spiked chamomile tea, wearing party hats. Then, nervous about our double homicide, we could lie in bed together, holding hands again, frightened and waiting, still wondering, after all these years, if we even believed in our own souls.
explicacao: Citação de Timothy Schaffert sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “We could endlessly reminisce, live in the past to an unhealthy degree, then politely kill each ot…” articula reflexao, death-and-dying, old-age em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e luto.
